name: Sliced Pork with Wood Ear Fungus
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 oz Wood ear fungus, dried, soaked 25 minutes in warm water
1 1/2 oz Spinach, leaves young and tender
3 Red chili peppers, fresh or pickled/chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the pork across the grain into very thin slices and cut into bite-sized pieces.
2. Place the sliced pork in a dish with the seasoning #1 ingredients. Mix well and leave for 20 minutes.
3. Drain the wood ear fungus, and chop finely. Wash and dry the spinach leaves, then chop coarsely.
4. Heat a wok or large skillet, add the lard and oil. Stir-fry the green onions, garlic, and chili peppers together for one minute.
5. Add the wood ear fungus and spinach to the pan, stir-frying briefly.
6. Push the ingredients to one side of the pan, then add the sliced pork. Fry on both sides until lightly colored, about 1 1/4 minutes.
7. Stir in the seasoning #2 ingredients and continue to stir-fry until the pork is almost cooked.
8. Pour in the pre-mixed seasoning #2 ingredients and simmer until the sauce has thickened.
9. Add a dash of salt to taste, and serve with steamed rice.
